name: Ted Dabrowski
bio: Ted Dabrowski is the president of Wirepoints, a Chicago-based fiscal policy think tank known for research on Illinois pension debt, property taxes, and population loss. He is a first-time candidate for elected office. Born in Poland, he emigrated to the U.S. and built a career in finance and public policy. His candidacy argues Illinois's problems require structural fiscal reform — not just a party change — and that previous Republican nominees lacked a credible economic vision. He is the most policy-detailed candidate in the Republican field.

jacket.note: Policy-forward first-time candidate. Wirepoints reputation gives credibility with fiscal conservatives and media. Long-shot in primary but most detailed platform in the field.

red_flags.label: No Prior Elected Office
red_flags.detail: Dabrowski has never held elected office. While his policy expertise is respected, he has no track record as a political candidate or executive decision-maker.

policy_positions: Property tax cap phased in at 1% of assessed value
Veto all tax increases; use line-item vetoes to cut spending
Repeal zero-emissions energy mandate; return to fossil fuel and nuclear balance
401K-style retirement plans for all new government workers to stop pension debt growth
Cut Illinois corporate income tax and commercial property taxes
Reduce 280,000+ state regulations; 3-old-rules-cut for every new rule
School vouchers; end politically driven curriculum
